story.title: Dr. Nair's Great Matter Count
story.text:
My name is Dr. Priya Nair. I am a scientist who works with matter. My job is to make sure we don't lose any of it when we make new things.

Think of it like being a detective for tiny atoms! Everything is made of matter. Matter has mass, which is how much stuff is in it. We have a rule in science called conservation. It means matter can change, but it can’t just disappear.

Here is how I model it:
1. We start with a solid material. We measure its Starting Mass very carefully.
2. We add heat. The Air Temperature around it goes up.
3. The solid melts into a liquid. This is a physical change. The Ice Amount (our solid) goes down. The Liquid Water Amount goes up.

The total mass at the start and the end is exactly the same! The matter just changed its state of matter. Sometimes, a change makes a gas we can’t see. My job is to track that invisible gas, too. Every atom counts!

margin_defs.definition: The matter that something is made from.

check_prompts: What happens to the amount of solid when the temperature goes up?

vocabulary.definition: A science rule that says matter can't be made or lost, only changed.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Because of conservation, the total mass of the water stayed the same when it froze.

vocabulary.definition: The form that matter is in, like a solid, a liquid, or a gas.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Water's state of matter can change from liquid to solid ice.

vocabulary.definition: A change in how matter looks or feels, but not what it is made of.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Melting an ice cube is a physical change.

vocabulary.definition: The amount of stuff, or matter, that is in an object.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A big rock has more mass than a small pebble.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Nair mentioned that some changes create a gas. How could you prove that a gas has mass, even if you can't see it?
discussion_cards.frame: To prove a gas has mass, I could try to...

discussion_cards.prompt: Why is it important for a scientist like Dr. Nair to track all the matter and make sure none is wasted?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important to not waste matter because...

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
